frameworks.name: renormalization group
frameworks.one_line: Phenomenological coarse-graining of neural activity to probe fixed-point structure.

frameworks.name: critical phenomena
frameworks.one_line: Scaling and non-Gaussian fixed-point distributions as signatures of criticality.

frameworks.name: collective network dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Population activity treated as an emergent collective state.

core_question: Does the collective activity of a large neural population exhibit a nontrivial fixed point under coarse-graining?
key_assumptions: Neural population activity admits a phenomenological coarse-graining procedure analogous to block-spin RG.
Large simultaneously recorded populations can reveal scaling laws if present.
Fixed-point structure can be diagnosed from distributions and dynamic quantities.
manipulations: Level of coarse-graining applied to the neural population.
Static vs dynamic quantities examined under successive coarse-grainings.
key_findings: Distributions of coarse-grained variables approach a fixed non-Gaussian form.
Evidence of scaling appears in both static and dynamic quantities.
interpretations: The collective behavior of the hippocampal network is described by a nontrivial fixed point.
